title: 'I'm going to die': Guard recalls supervisor's distress call in Mira Road stabbing case
content: According to police officials, the accused approached the guards stationed at an under-construction building near Wockhardt Hospital in the early hours of Monday, around 4.00 am. He reportedly asked them to recite the Kalma, an important Islamic declaration of faith, before launching the attack. Police moved swiftly after the attack, scanning CCTV footage from the area to trace the suspect. Ansari was tracked down and apprehended within roughly 90 minutes. He was later taken to Naya Nagar Police Station, where a case was registered against him under charges including attempted murder and promoting enmity between communities.

title: Shocking! Riyan Parag captured vaping inside dressing room during PBKS vs RR
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was filmed vaping in the team's dressing room during a live broadcast, sparking controversy. The act is illegal in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019, raising concerns about athlete health and regulatory compliance. This incident follows a previous breach of protocol by the RR manager.

title: Threat or theater? Trump assassination bid case turns into political noir
content: US authorities have charged a suspect in an alleged third assassination attempt on President Trump during a White House Correspondents' dinner security breach. The incident has polarized the nation, with conservatives citing escalating political violence and liberals expressing skepticism. Late-night hosts' jokes have further fueled tensions, prompting White House calls for regulatory scrutiny of networks.

title: China gives two week deadline to Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta to abort $2bn Manus deal
content: China has ordered Meta to reverse its $2.5 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus within weeks, citing national security. Beijing claims authority over the Singapore-based firm's China-linked AI technology, demanding the return of assets and data. This move signals a strong warning to Chinese tech founders against exporting sensitive innovations to geopolitical rivals.

title: Rajasthan Royals hand Punjab Kings first defeat as Ferreira’s fifty seals 6-wicket win
content: Donovan Ferreira and Shubham Dubey's unbeaten 77-run partnership off 32 balls secured a thrilling six-wicket victory for Rajasthan Royals against Punjab Kings. Earlier, 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rapid 43 off 16 balls set the tone for the chase, paving the way for RR's successful pursuit of 223.

title: Accident victim’s kin forced to pay and wash 108 ambulance in MP; video sparks outrage
content: Relatives of a critically injured road accident victim in Katni were allegedly forced to pay and clean a 108 ambulance before his transfer for further treatment. A viral video showed a family member washing the vehicle, prompting an inquiry and the termination of two employees by the ambulance operating agency.

title: Ladakh groups seek Amit Shah meeting during Leh visit
content: Ladakh's political groups are seeking a meeting with Union home minister Amit Shah during his upcoming visit to press for Sixth Schedule status and statehood.  They deem the Centre's announced sub-committee meeting inadequate and hope Shah's presence will facilitate a high-powered committee discussion to rebuild trust and address their demands.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: AAP MLA Mehraj Malik released from jail after J&K HC quashes PSA detention
content: AAP MLA Mehraj Malik was released from Kathua jail after eight months following a J&K High Court order quashing his detention under the Public Safety Act. The court found the detention legally unsustainable. Malik, AAP's J&K president, was detained on charges of disturbing public order.

title: Iltija Mufti protests removal of Urdu from job recruitment eligibility rules, slams NC govt
content: Iltija Mufti, a PDP leader, protested the government's plan to remove Urdu knowledge as a requirement for Jammu and Kashmir Revenue Service jobs. She stated this move attacks the region's linguistic heritage. Mufti argued Urdu connects communities and is a repository of knowledge. She questioned the government's priorities and vowed to resist the changes.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 41: Ranveer Singh film eyes 9 year record of 'Baahubali 2'
content: On April 28, 2026, ‘Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge’ reached a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,779.02 crore, placing it just Rs. 10.54 crore away from beating ‘Baahubali 2’ to become the second highest-grossing Indian film globally. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, the film’s India net stands at Rs. 1,131.83 crore, continuing its pursuit of ‘Pushpa 2’s domestic record. Despite the IPL 2026 season and the release of ‘Bhooth Bangla,’ the espionage thriller remains a box office juggernaut.

title: What global banks said about Anthropic's Mythos that company ‘refused’ to release publicly
content: Anthropic's powerful AI model, Claude Mythos, capable of finding software vulnerabilities, has prompted a global financial sector scramble. Banks, regulators, and central bankers are urgently assessing risks and accelerating cybersecurity investments. Project Glasswing offers controlled access for defensive security work, highlighting the evolving threat landscape.

title: J&K court acquits wife, her 'partner’ of husband’s murder over lack of proof
content: A court in Bandipora, Jammu and Kashmir, acquitted a woman and her alleged partner of murder charges due to insufficient evidence. The judge highlighted the prosecution's failure to produce a crucial forensic report, which was necessary to determine the cause of death. The court emphasized that convictions require proof, not mere suspicion.

title: The Phase 2 trust vote in West Bengal: Who will win the Matua votes?
content: Electoral analysts estimate that Matua voters influence outcomes in 60 to 65 assembly seats. In the second phase alone, they are a key factor in 21 constituencies. In tightly contested elections, this scale of influence gives the community leverage that political parties cannot ignore. This is evident in recent electoral trends.

title: IPL Points Table: RR move to third after handing PBKS first defeat of the season
content: Rajasthan Royals achieved a historic six-wicket victory over league leaders Punjab Kings, marking PBKS's first defeat in IPL 2026. This win propelled RR to third place with 12 points, while PBKS remains atop the table. The result also saw Sunrisers Hyderabad slip to fourth.

title: Jason Whitlock links Dianna Russini to Vrabel, Tomlin & McVay via fast food
content: Former FOX Sports Radio host Jason Whitlock linked top NFL reporter Dianna Russini with NFL coaches Mike Vrabel, Mike Tomlin, and Sean McVay. He drew parallels to fast food chains. He referenced an old X post by Steve Levy. Levy praised Samantha Ponder for noticing Russini’s interview success with two NFL coaches during COVID-19 restrictions. Whitlock also recalled a past statement by Dianna Russini. He interpreted it as a confession about her true aspirations.

title: Fred Liu - Evelyn Ha’s ex backs Ben Pasternak, alleges abuse details
content: Fred Liu, Evelyn Ha’s ex-boyfriend, has supported parts of Ben Pasternak’s abuse claims after his arrest in New York. Pasternak denies all charges and says Ha was the aggressor. Liu shared that his past experience felt similar but stayed silent earlier. The case includes serious assault charges and is ongoing, with court proceedings expected on June 11.

title: MrBeast sues MHQ for $5M over Beast Games legal dispute
content: MrBeast’s legal dispute with MHQ has become one of the biggest behind-the-scenes stories around Beast Games. While his company wants its $5 million returned, MHQ says it helped build the project from the start and suffered major business damage after the deal collapsed. The final court decision is still pending.

title: US-Iran war impact on India: Why RBI is cautious yet confident
content: For RBI the situation is clear: The resilience of the global economy, already inflicted with trade tensions, is being tested by the conflict in West Asia. The biggest takeaway from the RBI bulletin is that India’s domestic supply chains may come under risk from a prolonged war scenario, though strong macroeconomic fundamentals provide a buffer.

title: Jimmy Kimmel's inspiring quote on making a difference
content: Veteran comedian Jimmy Kimmel, a fixture in late-night television for over two decades, shared profound wisdom with future doctors. He emphasized that while not everyone can be saved, actively making the world a better place daily is the true essence of their profession. This perspective offers a release from perfection's pressure, highlighting the cumulative impact of consistent, intentional efforts.

title: Is US leaving Nato? Why Spain and Iran war could trigger Trump’s biggest global shock
content: Fresh reports reveal internal Pentagon discussions exploring punitive measures against Nato allies, including Spain, for perceived lack of support during the Iran conflict. These discussions, alongside President Trump's continued questioning of US commitment, expose deep divisions within the alliance, raising questions about its future and European security.

title: Rohit Sharma injury update: MI keep fans guessing ahead of SRH clash at Wankhede
content: Rohit Sharma's participation in Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 match against Sunrisers Hyderabad is uncertain. A final decision will be made at the toss. Sharma has been recovering from a hamstring injury. Suryakumar Yadav is preparing intensely for the game. Players from both teams were seen interacting during training sessions. Mumbai Indians await a potential boost for this key home contest.

title: Mamata's big test: Will brand 'Didi' deliver again in Bengal?
content: Mamata Banerjee's 'Didi' brand faces its toughest test in West Bengal's elections, balancing welfare schemes and grassroots appeal against BJP's national push.  Despite corruption allegations and voter fatigue, her maternalist state model and street-fighter persona remain key, as she rallies voters against perceived disenfranchisement and external forces.

title: Koovagam festival: Where transgenders flock to wed, celebrate and mourn - photos
content: Thousands of transgender women, dressed in fine silk saris and adorned with traditional jewellery and flowers, participated in a symbolic mass wedding.

title: IPL 2026: Why Punjab Kings wore black armbands against Rajasthan Royals
content: Punjab Kings players donned black armbands against Rajasthan Royals, honouring teammate Azmatullah Omarzai's late mother. Captain Shreyas Iyer conveyed heartfelt condolences, emphasising team solidarity. Punjab posted a formidable 222/4, showcasing resilience and unity in a high-scoring IPL 2026 encounter.

title: 'Politics has its place': Akhilesh visits BJP MLA who was injured while burning his effigy
content: SP leader Akhilesh Yadav visited BJP MLA Anupama Jaiswal in a Lucknow hospital after she sustained severe facial burns while attempting to set fire to an effigy. Yadav expressed his wishes for her speedy recovery, emphasizing that political differences should not overshadow compassion and human relationships. Jaiswal's husband lauded the visit as an act of "human sensitivity and democratic courtesy."

title: Mirzapur film: Divyenndu promises a full paisa vasool ride
content: Mirzapur: The Film is easily among the most anticipated projects of the year, and the excitement has only intensified with Divyenndu Sharma returning as Munna Bhaiya. With fans closely tracking every update, the actor has now shared a glimpse of what’s in store—and it sounds massive.

title: Watch: Virat Kohli fan breaks down after failing to get autograph in viral video
content: A young Virat Kohli fan's heartbreaking reaction to a missed autograph opportunity outside the RCB hotel has gone viral during IPL 2026. The boy's tearful outburst after being unable to meet his idol highlights the immense fan adoration for the star India batter, who is also enjoying a record-breaking season.

title: Chris Simms on Russini-Vrabel buzz as Mike Tomlin takes NBC Sports job
content: An alleged Sedona cheating scandal involving top NFL reporter Dianna Russini and New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel has led to significant fallout in the media industry. Russini resigned from The Athletic, Crisy Froyd was fired for extending her support to Russini, and Chris Simms lost his NBC Sports job after impatiently hinting at the story. Interestingly, Pittsburgh Steelers longtime head coach Mike Tomlin, who previously granted Russini interview during COVID-19 restrictions has now joined NBC Sports in place of Simms.

title: Greece: 89-yr-old held after shootings at social security office, courthouse, 4 injured
content: An 89-year-old man was apprehended in Patras after allegedly injuring at least four people in two separate shootings in Athens. Authorities believe pension grievances and psychological issues fueled the attacks, which targeted a social security office and a court building. The suspect, found with a weapon, is under investigation.

title: Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of killing husband during honeymoon gets bail
content: A Meghalaya court granted bail to Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused in the 2025 honeymoon killing of her husband, Raja Raghuvanshi. His mutilated body was found in June last year. Police had filed a charge sheet naming eight individuals, including Sonam and her alleged boyfriend, in connection with the transport businessman's death.

title: UAE to exit OPEC, OPEC+ amid Hormuz disruptions: What it means for world oil order
content: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries or OPEC is a group of major oil-producing nations. This group coordinates their petroleum production policies to help effectively manage global oil supply and influence crude prices.

title: 'If he's Singham, I am Pushpa': TMC protests over IPS Ajay Sharma's warning on voter intimidation
content: Trinamool Congress supporters in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas protested against an Election Commission-appointed police observer, Ajay Pal Sharma. They accused him of intimidating party workers ahead of the second phase of polling. Sharma had warned of strict action against those influencing voters, prompting a strong response from TMC candidate Jahangir Khan.

title: Rani Kapur reacts as SC urges mediation in Rs 30,000 crore dispute
content: The Supreme Court urged mediation in the estate dispute of late businessman Sanjay Kapur, noting his mother Rani Kapur's age. Rani welcomed the court's suggestion for a peaceful resolution, seeking truth and protection of her rightful assets. The court emphasized mediation as a better alternative to prolonged legal battles.

title: Watch: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi wins heart with humble gesture for young fan
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, fresh off a century against Sunrisers Hyderabad, arrived at the Rajasthan Royals team hotel. Despite his recent success, he displayed humility by gifting his welcome garland to a young fan.  The team is preparing to face Punjab Kings in Mullanpur.

title: IPL 2026: PBKS unleash Lockie Ferguson as pace weapon; RR opt to bowl
content: Punjab Kings bolstered their bowling with the formidable Lockie Ferguson for their IPL 2026 clash against Rajasthan Royals.  Rajasthan won the toss and chose to bowl, aiming to exploit early conditions.  Unbeaten Punjab seek to extend their winning streak, while fourth-placed Rajasthan eye a climb up the table with a strong performance.

title: MEA condemns Mali terror attacks, calls for regional stability
content: India has strongly condemned the "terrorist attacks" in Mali, extending condolences to victims and expressing solidarity with the nation. The Ministry of External Affairs also mourned the death of Mali's Defence Minister in the assault. This coordinated militant offensive, the largest in over a decade, has significantly worsened security, despite Russian support for the military government.

title: ‘Trusting batters down the order’: Manjrekar reveals key to Kohli’s transformation
content: Virat Kohli's T20 batting transformation this IPL season is attributed to a key realisation: he's no longer indispensable.  Sanjay Manjrekar notes Kohli now bats quicker, trusting teammates and abandoning the anchor role. This shift, boosting his strike rate significantly, has unlocked RCB's potential, proving modern T20 demands aggression over individual longevity.

title: ‘Just peace’ can’t be found by joining organisations that charge fees: UNGA President defends UN system
content: Ms. Baerbock cautioned against ‘unilateral use of force’ while arguing that member states of the UN can mobilise themselves for an institutional action against a state that is accused of genocide by bringing the matter to the UN Security Council

title: IPL 2026 RR vs PBKS: Ferreira and Shubham combine to end Punjab Kings’ unbeaten streak
content: The two plunder 77 off 32 balls for the unbroken fifth wicket to help Rajasthan Royals chase down the 223-run target; JaiSurya pair sizzles yet again

title: CITU petitions ILO over ‘suppression of workers’ rights by Centre and Uttar Pradesh governments’
content: ‘Government of India and the Uttar Pradesh Government are in violation of fundamental ILO principles’

title: J&K govt. blacklists Reliance General Insurance Company Limited for 2 years
content: According to the government, the insurance company failed to issue health insurance cards, provide the list of empanelled hospital, web-based application platform and also failed to finalize performance parameters and penalty framework forming part of the contractual obligations

title: EC asks NIA to ensure bombs not used to disrupt Bengal polls on April 29
content: The directive comes after recovery of crude bombs in the poll-bound State and reports of a minor blast

title: CBI official, ex-ACP get 3-month jail in 26-year-old assault case
content: The case pertains to a controversial search operation conducted on October 19, 2000, at the house of Indian Revenue Service officer Ashok Kumar Aggarwal

title: Dhayanidhi Alagiri approaches Madras High Court against attachment of properties by ED
content: Dhayanidhi’s father M.K. Alagiri filed an affidavit on his behalf and said his son “suffers from intellectual incapacity, remains partially paralysed, and lacks the medical ability to swear the affidavit”

title: ED provisionally attaches ₹1,113-crore assets in Raheja Developers case
content: The ED is conducting an investigation against Raheja Developers Ltd., its director Navin M. Raheja, and other associated persons

title: BJP sweeps local body polls in Gujarat; wins 15 Municipal Corporations, 33 District Panchayats
content: BJP has won over half of the total seats in the municipal corporations of Surendranagar, Jamnagar, Porbandar, Karamsad-Anand, Nadiad, Navsari, Vapi, Surat and Morbi.

title: Baby boy found in Ammathottil in Kerala’s Thiruvananthapuram
content: The boy was received in the electronic cradle set up by the Kerala State Council for Child Welfare for safely receiving abandoned newborns

title: SC/ST communities in Karnataka continue to face land-related issues, finds study
content: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in Karnataka continue to suffer in land-related issues, despite legislations being in place, a study has said

title: Air India, IndiGo, SpiceJet seek ATF price relief; say airline industry on verge of ‘stopping operations’
content: The West Asia turmoil has pushed up oil prices, and airspace restrictions have increased airlines' operating costs, especially on long-haul routes

title: Internal quota: Karnataka govt. notifies new ratio
content: While the Karnataka government has notified the new ratio slicing up 15% reservation among the 101 Scheduled Castes in the State, doubts have been expressed over how the roster points system and introduction of “Scheduled Castes General” would practically work out

title: Maharashtra ATS begins probe into Thane knife attack
content: The police say they have recovered handwritten notes allegedly indicating the desire of the accused to join the Islamic State, a banned terrorist organisation

title: PDP protests over Urdu, says dropping language as eligibility an ‘attack on Kashmir’s identity’
content: A Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) verdict stayed the operation of provisions of the J&K Revenue (Subordinate) Service Recruitment Rules of 2009, which made knowledge of Urdu as the minimum qualification, in the Revenue department; Opposition PDP criticises NC govt. over the decision

title: Shillong court grants bail to Sonam Raghuvanshi
content: She is the prime accused in the murder of her Indore-based businessman-husband, Raja Raghuvanshi, during their honeymoon trip to Meghalaya

title: Karnataka High Court notice to CBI on Vinay Kulkarni’s appeal against conviction in murder case
content: The High Court of Karnataka on Tuesday ordered issue of notice to the Central Bureau of Investigation on a petition filed by Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni challenging a special court’s order of convicting him in a murder case

title: On TCS harassment and conversion case
content: Concerns have been raised about the Tata Consultancy Services sexual exploitation and alleged forcible conversions case that involves nine FIRs, related to complaints of alleged sexual exploitation, rape and forcible conversion at the company’s office in Nashik, Maharashtra, between 2022 and 2026

title: Karnataka Chief Minister ready to ‘sacrifice power or continue’ depending on high command’s decision, says Rajanna
content: Amid speculation over a leadership change in Karnataka, K.N. Rajanna, a close aide of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, has said the latter was willing to ‘either sacrifice power or continue in office’ as directed by the Congress high command and Rahul Gandhi

title: Odisha acts fast after tribal man carries sister’s remains to bank
content: Asked by the bank to submit proof of his sister’s death to withdraw money from her account, the tribal man walked the streets carrying her skeletal remains on his shoulder, a disturbing image that went viral and sparked widespread outrage

title: IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma was not on EC’s initial observer list for West Bengal
content: Trinamool Congress has accused 2011-batch IPS officer of Uttar Pradesh cadre, also a trained dental surgeon, of intimidating and threatening locals in Falta under West Bengal’s Diamond Harbour constituency.

title: Power restrictions in Kerala due to government’s own negligence: Chennithala
content: Congress leader alleges that Pinarayi govt. cancelled two long-term power agreements signed by UDF govt., on trivial grounds, thereby pushing people of Kerala into permanent hardship

title: Sikkim’s organic farming is a template for the country: PM
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urges tourists to set aside 5% of their budget to buy local products while visiting the Himalayan State

title: Pariwarvaadi parties not willing to permit Indian women to reach Assembly, Parliament: PM
content: PM vows he will “leave no stone unturned” to ensure that women’s right to reservation in legislative bodies is implemented

title: Reschedule work hours, provide drinking water to workers: Centre’s advisory to States on heat wave
content: According to the statement, the Ministry has urged all States/UTs and their organisations to take immediate preventive and mitigation measures to safeguard workers and labourers from the adverse effects of rising temperatures and heatwave conditions across the country

title: Police file FIR against viral video of pro-Pakistan posters put up in J&K’s Doda
content: Posters praising Pakistani PM, Army Chief’s role in U.S.-Iran peace talks were pasted on a Doda bridge;  police trying to verify origin, authenticity of viral video

title: Alleged drug trafficking kingpin deported from Türkiye to India
content: 59-year-old accused Salim Dola was handed over to the Narcotics Control Bureau and taken to its headquarters in Delhi

title: A grief divided: funeral held on the Kishanganga’s banks prompts calls to reopen LoC crossing points
content: Siblings of the dead Kupwara resident, who have lived in POK since 1989, bid farewell to their brother from across the river; LoC crossing points used by such divided families were shut by the Centre in 2019

title: West Bengal goes to the polls in the second phase of Assembly election
content: The 142 seats going to the polls in the second phase are spread across seven districts of south Bengal, including Kolkata; about 3.21 crore electors will determine the fate of 1,448 candidates in the fray

title: Police arrest ‘ISI-backed Khalistanis who triggered low intensity bomb on railway track’
content: Interrogation revealed that the accused conducted a low-intensity IED blast at the main line of the Shambhu railway station

title: Watch: UAE shocks world with dramatic OPEC exit | Above the Fold | 28.04.2026
content: From the UAE’s OPEC exit to India’s heatwave, SCO meet updates and the Jakarta train crash, we bring you the top stories that matter with clarity and context.

title: Karnataka High Court stays CERC’s new regulations imposing higher penalty for deviation on renewable energy firms
content: The petitioners argued that the new framework is draconian and scientifically unfounded, given the inherently unpredictable, weather-dependent nature of solar and wind power generation

title: "Was Even-Tempered, Calm": Former Classmate To NDTV On Trump Event Shooter
content: Regarding his views on politics, Elizabeth Terlinden said that she knew the accused through the Caltech Christian Fellowship. She said that their main disagreement about politics happened only because he thought she "spent too much time" on politics

title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump Dissatisfied With Iran Proposal To Lift Hormuz Blockade
content: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif during a phone call Saturday night that the US "should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade"

title: 89-Year-Old Gunman Arrested After Mass Shooting In Athens, At Least 4 Wounded
content: The suspect who was armed with a shotgun wounded an employee at the social security office. By the time police officers arrived at the scene, the gunman was gone.

title: Fish To Jhalmuri, Identity To SIR: Issues That Shaped Bengal Polls
content: West Bengal assembly elections 2026: NDTV's power-packed team explored the politics of jhalmuri, fish, infiltration, and women's security

title: 'Only Asked For Papers': Odisha Bank After Man Goes With Sister's Skeleton
content: A shocking clip of the man carrying the remains to the Maliposi branch of Odisha Grameen Bank on Monday has gone viral on social media.

title: Transgenders Have No Legal Right To Demand Traditional 'Badhai': High Court
content: The court also noted that the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019, does not provide for any such right.

title: Married UP Woman Climbs Mobile Tower, Says Wants To Be With Her Lover: Cops
content: A married woman climbed a mobile tower and created a dramatic scene for nearly three hours in Uttar Pradesh's Hathras district on Tuesday, allegedly to be with her lover, prompting a swift response from police and fire officials.

title: Election Commission Transfers Bengal Poll Officer After 'Singham' Officer Row
content: A poll body official said the Joint BDO, Sourav Hazra, has been moved out of Falta and posted to Purulia with immediate effect.

title: "Return Of The Pirates": Iran's 'Plunder' Jibe At US After It Seizes Tankers
content: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said the operation effectively endorsed piracy. He described it as "the outright legalisation of piracy and armed robbery on the high seas".

title: "No One Can End Reservation": Uma Bharti's Strong Pitch In Madhya Pradesh
content: Uma Bharti delivered an unapologetically hardline defence of reservation, declaring that the system cannot and should not end until India's deepest social inequalities are dismantled

title: Minister-IAS Officer Dispute Reaches Chief Minister's Door In Madhya Pradesh
content: Madhya Pradesh is increasingly witnessing a disturbing pattern of friction between politicians and the bureaucracy, with a series of controversies now raising larger questions about governance, administrative discipline, and the balance of power.

title: Jahangir Khan: Rural Politician To Bengal Talking Point After 'Singham' Row
content: Khan -- the candidate for the Falta constituency -- was one of the small-time politicians in rural Bengal, though well-known in his area, the South 24 Parganas. His rise started with the election of Abhishek Banerjee to Lok Sabha

title: AAP MP Balbir Seechewal, Who Didn't Quit, Traces The Roots Of Rebellion
content: AAP MP Balbir Singh Seechewal said the MPs who engineered the AAP implosion were partymen and not public leaders, who happened to "rule in the state like kings"

title: Heat That Doesn't Sleep: Nights Turn Warmer Across India Amid Heatwave
content: The India Meteorological Department has warned that "warm night" conditions are likely across parts of Delhi, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh.

title: ISI-Linked Khalistani Module Busted After Blast Near Rail Tracks In Punjab
content: The swift operation led to the arrest of four highly radicalised individuals and the recovery of a significant cache of weapons and explosive material.

title: Gaming, Rants, Hitler Comparisons: What Trump Event Shooter Posted Online
content: According to CNN, Allen had shared posts comparing Trump to Hitler and even called upon others who criticised his presidency to purchase guns. His activity on social media ranged from posts about video games to angry political messages.

title: WhatsApp Banned 9,400-Plus Accounts In 12 Weeks Over Digital Arrest Fraud
content: WhatsApp said its investigation found a large number of accounts targeting Indian users were being operated from southeast Asia, particularly Cambodia.

title: Neighbourhood Schools Must Admit Students Without Delay: Supreme Court
content: The bench said earnest implementation can truly be transformative and it is not only a step towards educating young India, but also a substantive measure in securing the preambular objective of 'equality of status'.

title: New DNA study reshapes understanding of how humans evolved
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More

title: ‘When you clean your house, few glasses break’: Subrata Thakur’s SIR defence in Matua heartland
content: Subrata Thakur is the grandson of P R Thakur and matua matriarch Baroma Binapani Devi . he is the sitting MLA and BJP candidate for the election . the election is trickier than the earlier ones for him and other BJP leaders .

title: Trump claims Iran collapse while Teheran sees leverage as Hormuz stand-off tightens
content: the strait of Hormuz is a hardened maritime standoff in which Tehran demands the US lift its naval blockade . the impasse is now stressing out economies across the world, including in the united states itself . analysts tracking the crisis say the facts point not to unilateral Iranian capitulation but to a mutual coercion strategy .

title: The red string and the widow: Mystery of the missing scientists in US
content: the internet found a bone in the shape of a common denominator that connects it all . by mid-April 2026, the deaths and disappearances of eleven people with ties to US national security had become a full-blown phenomenon . the white house fielded questions; congress demanded answers .
